reviews.text: That's a great apartment!
Great Apartment! Great price value and enjoyed my stay there!

address: 1002 N. Aurora St. Unit 1 , Ithaca, NY
1005 - 1007 N Aurora St Unit 1, Ithaca, NY
101 N. Quarry Unit C, Ithaca, NY
101 N. Quarry St Unit A, Ithaca, NY
103 Birdseye View Dr near Danny rd

reviews.text: Apartment is super cute and cozy. 3 bathrooms is great! Definitely needs to be maintained better. Very old appliances and windows.

address: 103 N. Quarry St Unit A, Ithaca, NY
104-06 N Aurora St near E State street

reviews.text: Loved this place
I love living here and re-signed. Place is gorgeous and one of the best I’ve seen.

I've loved living here and I've had an amazing experience with the landlords as well. Despite being far, it is a great price and the bus stop makes up for it. I'm planning to stay for the next 2 years :))
Very quiet and peaceful, laundry is convenient shared btw 5, landlord is responsive, no additional fees besides utilities

I lived in Apt 3 for 3 years and it was an overall positive experience. Monica and Simon, the old landlords were great, but now it’s under new management. The new landlords seem ok but definitely less responsive. The location is far, but it helps that there is a bus stop right across the street. Heating was spotty across the apartment as some rooms were better than others, but a space heater helped. Other than that the space was huge and room sizes were good. Rooms closest to the road could hear some road noise sometimes but it wasn’t too bad. Walls are a bit thin, but it’s pretty standard in Ithaca. Overall great experience and would highly recommend living here, especially if you have a solid group of 5!
Comfortable rooms, amazing in-house gym!
Overall great place!! The only issues is that it can get too cold, the thermostat can be broken sometimes and laundry can be quite sensitive. But communication and maintenance is great and quick!!

The 2 gyms that they have are great !
The location is great !
Overall it's clean.
However, the heating system is too noisy I couldn't even sleep over the whole winter and more.

I think overall it's pretty good but during COVID-19, they are not being very understanding. For example, many international students weren't able to travel back to Ithaca and they hoped to defer or terminate the lease. However, the CTT staff won't allow termination of lease at all and we must sublet the house with a lower rate by ourselves. At the same time, they allowed certain tenants to defer the lease, which is very unfair and confusing.
The gym is great. This place is very clean and quiet too. Also, the shuttle that goes to wegmans and campus make our life so much easier.
Amazing living experience
Great place with free gym and shuttle to campus at a reasonable prices, but the location is a little inconvenient if you don’t have a car
Very clean and great quality, but a bit far from Campus.
Safe neighborhood, quite good maintenance.

I think overall it's pretty good but during COVID-19, they are not being very understanding. For example, many international students weren't able to travel back to Ithaca and they hoped to defer or terminate the lease. However, the CTT staff won't allow termination of lease at all and we must sublet the house with a lower rate by ourselves. At the same time, they allowed certain tenants to defer the lease, which is very unfair and confusing.
Very clean and great quality, but a bit far from Campus.
Stayed here during the summer. Great overall. Very convenient in-house washer and dryer. Nice kitchen. Occasional blackouts and elevator problems.

CTT is the perfect place to live in terms of facilities and price, if not considering accessibility. However, there is a shuttle which runs every 20 min in the morning (used to be 10 min pre-covid) and once every hour in the afternoon until 4:15pm. There's also a dryer and a washing machine in your room.

The landlord sometimes seems to be predatory, even when it comes to your lease. They openly say that they will rent your apartment out from under you without notification. I assume as a play to attempt to get tenants to renew their leases. I wouldn't bite at any of these tactics.

Beyond this, I haven't had any problems with safety or cleanliness. They have covered parking under the building, which is my main reason for choosing the apartment.

The location is a bit far from Ctown. Maintenance is very quick! AC & in-unit washer and dryer is great. Living in 2B/2B and the kitchen is a bit small. Living in an A2 apartment style on Valentine so I have a walk-in closet and Queen queen-size bed.

The cons is that it is bit far away from the campus. I can not go back to my house if I leave something there since it takes more than 40 minutes to go back and come. Also, there is no concierge, so we have to manually pick up our package. The pro is that price is reasonable and cheap relative to its fairly good facilities. I love how it is modern and the laundry system is inside house. This is very convenient.

Collegetown Terrace is a steal!! Theres a bunch of gyms and super big and clean. The only issue is that the laundry is very sensitive, the thermostat can be shifty to work with and communication about inspections isn’t great.
Overall great place!! The only issues is that it can get too cold, the thermostat can be broken sometimes and laundry can be quite sensitive. But communication and maintenance is great and quick!!

The cons is that it is bit far away from the campus. I can not go back to my house if I leave something there since it takes more than 40 minutes to go back and come. Also, there is no concierge, so we have to manually pick up our package. The pro is that price is reasonable and cheap relative to its fairly good facilities. I love how it is modern and the laundry system is inside house. This is very convenient.

Too expensive for ithaca! 
Everything is good in the new construction, but maybe a little too pricy for ithaca.

Would like better renovation in the older units and maybe like maple wood make it easier and include utility in rents
Very nice place. Got 2 gyms and a big common area where people can hang out or study. Definitely worth the rent.

CTT is a very nice apartment complex. Some people might think it's too far away from campus, but it's not too bad if you use the shuttle bus that runs every 10 minutes in the morning. The price is relatively reasonable compared to the apartments in Collegetown, we have huge parking spaces, there are washer-dryer units built in, there are compost grinders in every kitchen sink.
I think terrace is super quiet and peaceful. It is a little far from campus, but there is a shuttle service every 10min in the morning up to campus, so it’s very convenient!
great apartment. On the expensive side but in a good location with nice landlords and quick maintenance

Overall, it's a very good location. The apartment is average quality, nothing special but also nothing really bad either. I'm moving to another apartment next semester because this 3 bedroom apartment is too small for 5 people, and all of us wanted our own rooms. It's pretty good for the rent we pay, but obviously it comes at a cost.

Overall, it's a very good location. The apartment is average quality, nothing special but also nothing really bad either. I'm moving to another apartment next semester because this 3 bedroom apartment is too small for 5 people, and all of us wanted our own rooms. It's pretty good for the rent we pay, but obviously it comes at a cost.

The apartment has been a pretty great experience overall. The building has very fast maintenance. The prices are reasonable, and I personally live in a unit with extra large bedrooms, which has an extra charge, but is still within reason. The apartment is fully furnished, with laundry, kitchen, internet access, and air conditioning all provided. Note the internet access is Wifi, but you have to bring your own router with an ethernet cable. Internet access is a one time separate charge. Trash is also a one time separate charge. Electricity and air conditioning are separately charged, and you will have to work with a separate company regarding those payments. The laundry is not free, rather it is a separate charge for washing machines and dryers, which is similar to how it is on campus. The building is right in Collegetown across the street from Kung Fu Tea and Mango Mango, and the building is a two minute walk to campus. It is also an extremely quiet area with no noise. The landlord is great, with a pretty quick response time to emails and maintenance requests. In terms of subletting, the landlord makes the process very streamlined, and there is no extra fee charged to the tenants. Subletting over the summer becomes a little difficult because the lease dates are not fully aligned with the beginning and end of the summer. Also, make sure to tour the place before you sign, as some parts of the building are renovated, while others are not.

I feel like I live pretty close to everyone I know. It's not that far from campus either. I really like having my own kitchen and bathroom. It's so close to the engineering quad and Starbucks, so it's been really convenient
pretty solid place! great location, relatively quick maintenance, overall a nice place to live. some variation between units tho so try to get a renovated one
Some of the appliances are outdated. Because our landlord quit, there is no one that really makes sure our packages are secure as they are left at the very front door.
Very good location. Not many additional amenities but the apartments are fairly large and are in fairly decent condition. Could probably find something of the same value for cheaper.
Great location, subpar condition and communication.
I really enjoy living here. Building is well maintained and I can walk to everything in College Town. I wish it was a bit quieter.
Really nice place in ctown and new landlord responds fast and is always at front now.
pretty good, elevator was broken for almost a year tho
Its a great location and apartment, just minor issues with noise and the building being pretty old.

Nice living experience with my best friends. We had a lot of great memories there.
But the move-out experience was terrible: because of COVID, my roommates and I all move out in March (2 months before the lease ended). We tried to negotiate a deduction of rent but the leasing company did nothing to help. Also, more than half of our deposit was deducted because of very expensive cleaning fees :(((

The location for Eddygate is unbelievably amazing because you can cut through CTB and be home from eng quad in 5 minutes, but the quality and state of furniture is pretty poor, we found our apt pretty dirty when we moved in. Also, we do not have a call system to let people into the building

I lived on the first floor in an apartment with 3 bedrooms. I had a great experience, and maintenance would respond fast if there were issues. The only con was that I lived right across Wings Over Ithaca, so it was really loud. But I had a balcony and the view was nice, so it was fine.

The location is wonderful, the heater is excellent, and all the essential services are provided (laundry, trash, bug extermination, etc.) The only thing is our wifi was a bit spotty sometimes because of the location of our apartment, but it was a minor inconvenience.

Apartment is so close to campus! Can get to eng quad/statler in less than 5 minutes which is a must! Walls inside apartment are a bit thin but not that bad. No overhead lighting but lamps help make it better!

address: 110 N Titus near S Cayuga St.
110 Parker Street near E. Seneca St

reviews.text: This place is horrible all apartments have too many people for the amount of space, the facilities are poor and not well cared for. I wouldn't recommend living here at all, I wish I had lived somewhere else this year.

Though this apartment has a great location in CTown, its overall condition is very questionable. In an apartment of 6 girls the kitchen is way too small, and there are frequently concerns with outlets in the apartment.

It is really convenient for campus, and nominally it is one of the cheapest you can get for this location; however, the value is definitely still not worth the rent. The apartment is aging, the bathrooms are designed weird, and the kitchen appliances are barely able to cook.
Cleanliness of apartment depends on which unity you lease. Highly recommend viewing the unit before leasing. Certain things such as shower door were broken.

Location can't get much better than this... in the heart of collegetown and the only downside about the location is that it can get a bit loud on weekends. A bit expensive for what you pay for, but I came from living in Dickson and the Gothics so in comparison, plaza is like heaven lol... Kitchen is quite small and not much counter space, and the bathroom leaves much to be desired, but overall not a bad place to live. I also think the conditions vary from apartment to apartment despite being in the same building... for example, my friend's bathroom a floor down is like super nice and modern but ours is a bit crusty. 

Also, bring an extra lamp. The lights are dim!

It is really convenient for campus, and nominally it is one of the cheapest you can get for this location; however, the value is definitely still not worth the rent. The apartment is aging, the bathrooms are designed weird, and the kitchen appliances are barely able to cook.
They gotta do some serious mold control
Extremely cramped and expensive. Too many people per suite. Smell of hot oil from Wings Over
Collegetown Plaza is a very cool place! Great price for a relatively new place. Would live there again.
I really enjoy living in plaza! it could be a little cheaper but it’s a great location so that’s worth it. 5 min walk to eng quad but could be a little updated

Despite the rather expensive pricing, the location (right in the heart of Collegetown) covers most of the small issues. Plus, although this might be a unique advantage of high floor apartments, the street noise is nearly inaudible.

A mid apartment with a great location. Way too expensive for the sub-par quality. Landlords treat the students a bit like children. Would recommend renting somewhere else overall, unless a short walk is vital. Make sure to see how small the apartment is before you rent; any single floor of a house will be double the size of something in plaza. You can find something better for a similar cost!

Elevator is down 1/3 of the time which is inconvenient because I live on the 5th floor (9). However my apartment is west facing so the view is very nice. 1 bath for 4 ppl is subpar. Gym is very small and sometimes AC doesn’t work in there. Rent has increased hella post COVID which sucks. Parking is super expensive. But apartment is close to boba so that’s a plus.
Cleanliness of apartment depends on which unity you lease. Highly recommend viewing the unit before leasing. Certain things such as shower door were broken.

If you get one of the west-facing rooms, the view of downtown Ithaca is amazing! It is very convenient for classes, especially if you are an engineer. The corporate management isn't very empathetic, but they usually have good communication for maintenance, packages, outages, etc. Laundry (located on the 5th floor) costs about the same as it does on-campus. You will need to bring your own router to use the Wi-Fi, which kinda sucks. The bathrooms are designed very weirdly, and will need to be maintained actively otherwise there will be mildew/mold. The kitchen is small with not a lot of counter or storage space. When the weather is nice, you will hear music playing from collegetown into the early morning on most nights.
Homey and good location and reasonable for the price, however it does get quite dingy and common areas smell really bad sometimes so maintenance could definitely improve.

Despite the rather expensive pricing, the location (right in the heart of Collegetown) covers most of the small issues. Plus, although this might be a unique advantage of high floor apartments, the street noise is nearly inaudible.
Pretty good tbh

This apartment is located in a great location in CTown, near many restaurants. There has been only 1 fire alarm during the whole semester so it's not that many as dorms. The maintenance communication seems pretty subjective. If they want to respond, they will very quickly, if not, you never receive any reason. However, as the office is right next to the apartment and open every day, you can visit it for any communication and pick up packages quickly.
Great location and management communication, but is quite expensive and the elevator breaks very often.

The apartment is good and I enjoy living here because of its closeness to the campus, good maintenance and closeness to restaurants/stores. The landlord are friendly and send out timely emails about important notices, package arrivals and any such details. While everything is comfortable, sometimes it feels a bit small compared to the price I pay. Overall, I enjoy living here and it's a great place for roommates.
I like living here. Close to campus. Safe neighborhood. Near retail stuff

I like staying at this apartment and have made myself at home here. I don't regret choosing this apartment and would choose to stay here again if I were a freshman again. The landlord and management are quite nice and did not do anything unethical. The location of this apartment is quite nice and we're close to a bus route. The only downside to this apartment is that it's dated. However, this apartment is comfortable and I like it here.
The landlord was super responsive which is rare in Ithaca. Also, parking was free with the rent which is a really great deal, especially since the apartment is farther away from campus.

Location sucks! Tough walk to campus, neighbors who don’t like noise. Landlord is standard, gives you full autonomy but the place is completely empty when you get it. Easy to make maintenance requests. Overall, a decent experience with PJ apts.
Washing machine is always breaking and eating my quarters

Lived in unit 4. It's conveniently located, quiet, and has in unit washing and drying. The kitchen space is huge with lots of cabinets, countertop space, two basin sink, 4 gas stove range with a hood, and a well conditioned oven. PJ apts are very good at responding quickly to maintenance requests or emergencies. The only potential downside is that you have to deal with the occasional spiders since unit 4 is by the attic. It can also get quite hot and you need to buy your own AC (they will install it for you).  Furthermore, the kitchen only fits a  mini fridge and mini freezer. So if you plan on cooking you most likely need to buy another mini fridge and put it in your room like I did.
Overall really good experience living here, the location is convenient and everything functioned fine.
Nice apartment, but cheaply made, attracts a party crowd. Super expensive and always trying to get more money from you.  Bedrooms are ridiculously small. Kitchen and bathrooms are decent, though.
great location! great amenities!

The AC has been broken since the summer (it is May) and there is a decent amount of noise from apartments above and below. They gave two different door codes to the roommates and communicated different things to us, leading to a lot of confusion. The washer also broke multiple times and you have to be careful running the washer and having the heat on, as it can trip the circuit breaker. Also the some windows don’t open at all (even in the bedroom) and the ventilation in the kitchen is bad, leading to the fire alarm triggering multiple times. There also were water leakages from the ceiling but maintenance quickly fixed it (although they did not come to retrieve their fan for three months) In unit washer dryer and dishwasher but you have to pay utilities and wifi. The landlord is somewhat responsive but the AC took so long to fix.
Landlords are quick to respond.
In-unit laundry is really convenient. Landlords are quick to respond. Very social and fun street! Apartment is a bit dated but a nice layout.
It happened to be next to a frat this year, 2024-2025, so it was loud most weekends
vert far from campus but cheap compared to other places with good maintenance
Amazing appartment
Pretty spacious, reasonable rent price, and an overall good place to live! It's pretty close to Collegetown so location is also convenient. Apartment was pretty clean when we moved in!

Overall my experience has been pretty good outside of maintenance issues. The location is very close to campus and the apartment is quite spacious for something at this price point. Furthermore, since the apartment is mirrored, we have two of everything, like kitchens, appliances, bathrooms, etc. which is quite convenient. If you're not worried about having the nicest apartment or dealing with maintenance, it is a fine apartment!
Awesome
Good place
Great location, central
Neutral
Good

address: 118 Prospect St, Cityview Apartment, room PHQ

reviews.text: Good value apartment not too far from campus. Communication is a little unorganized. Landlords don't have a great reputation.

The quality price ratio is very good. It's a quiet neighborhood and the layout is not bad. I can get to the main campus within 20 min walk and there's also a bus stop around. The room is not big but it's good enough for me. The landlord provides all necessary furnitures including a microwave, a fridge, and a tv. We don't need to pay anything extra except for the electricity bill. They are also super responsive to our maintenance requests. The layout is a bit weird. The bedrooms are right next to the living room so sometimes it can be a bit distracting when your roommates are hanging out while you need to do work, but this can happen in other places as well and I can just solve it with earbuds. Overall, not the best apartment out there but there's nothing bad about it. Plus the price is super good.

My roommates and I live on the top floor, where it's a 3 bedroom and 3 bathroom place, meaning everyone gets their own bathroom! We also have a loft in each room. I think that and the closeness to Collegetown/campus (near Eddy St.) adds a lot of value to this place, however, it has its own issues. I personally didn't have any issues with PJ apartments in communication apart from their odd key delivery system (they leave it under a specific doormat), but I have heard not-so-good things about them. However, there are a lot of issues in maintenance, like the fire escape not being secured and animals coming in, furniture being run-down, and a bit of mold in the bathroom that make me lower the review.
Nice location really close to campus! I live in a 3 bed 3 bath so it’s nice having my own bathroom. Conditions aren’t the best, but comparable to other houses in collegetown.
great location close to campus, clean, and quiet! not a lot of traffic, it’s a one way street.

address: 125 Farm Street near North Tioga St

reviews.text: Super outdated but homey place. Maintenance is super responsive. Also we have mice in our ceiling!
Nice place to live
Chill place to live 💯
Love this house! Old frat annex. Many problems and animals but we made it much better (girls lived here).
The apartment is in a great location. Great service and nice amenities. But the elevator breaks down a lot and maintenance is to reach.
Great location and apartments facing away from C Town have great views.
The location is very convenient, right in the center of Collegetown. Every unit also has an A.C. but maitenance can be a bit slow and furniture can be a bit old.
Nice Apartment! Recommended!

address: 200 Highland Ave - BTB Unit 2C '26-'27, Ithaca, NY 14850

reviews.text: The place is decent for the price and the location but overall the furniture is not too great. But the price got raised this year so its not as worth as before.
It's worth the price...
Definitely worth the price. Quality of apartment is not very high but expected at the cost of the rent and proximity to campus. Super convenient location.
Pretty good - kind of rough for corona since it is a little small at times and Ithaca collegetown kids do pull up outside late at night because it's on a more major road.
Dryden South has an amazing location in the center of ctown and is a very nice place to live!

The apartment is amazing for its relative price and location. It’s close to campus (besides a hill) and it has a very homey vibe. There are multiple units to the house, so your experience will vary based on your neighbors. The landlord is good on communication but is unavailable on weekends. Overall, great place to stay with friends, but the hill is a battle to go up.

It's nice and spacious. Ceilings are high so it makes it feel more home-y. Provided furniture is extremely comfortable and livable. Rooms are decent size with good doors and windows (they aren't broken) and each room has a closet space. Kitchen is decently large with a lot of cabinets and bathrooms are a little small but there are two full bathrooms which is really nice for a group of 4. In-unit laundry is amazing.

The apartment is amazing for its relative price and location. It’s close to campus (besides a hill) and it has a very homey vibe. There are multiple units to the house, so your experience will vary based on your neighbors. The landlord is good on communication but is unavailable on weekends. Overall, great place to stay with friends, but the hill is a battle to go up.
Beautiful building, nice living space with big windows. No issues with landlord or maintenance. Just a bit pricey.
super clean and modern, way better value than other apts in the area. but the laundry machines only take coins

I really enjoyed the fact that it came with plants, couch, and the whole furniture setup. I think the hill might be the worst part in terms of getting to the house but the house itself is nice. Really big closets, washer, dryer, dishwasher etc.

I really enjoyed the fact that it came with plants, couch, and the whole furniture setup. I think the hill might be the worst part in terms of getting to the house but the house itself is nice. Really big closets, washer, dryer, dishwasher etc.
The apartment is decently prices and the location is great. The only downside is that we need to physically go to the office to pick up our packages. Maintenance sometimes takes a long time.
It’s a budget apartment but it’s not that good

The location is very convenient and close to tcat stops/restaurants. Communication with maintenance can be delayed sometimes but repairs have always been addressed. Kitchen amenities are of moderate quality. When packages arrive, you must go to the Ithaca Renting offices to pick up.
It's very average. Furnitures are dorm looking like and if the kitchen isn't renovated, it's not the best. Some rooms are decent but some are small, and some units have small common space.
Super small and feels like a dorm but makes sense for the price. Live here if you miss on campus housing.

Pretty good, staff are friendly and responsive. The rent is pretty reasonable compare to other apartments in college town. Super convenient, everything is right downstairs. Need to pick up packages from ithaca renting office.
good

The location is amazing being 5 minutes away from the engineering quad and at the center of Collegetown. The building is a little old, but they fix maintenance requests within a day and are relatively responsive. There's also AC in the common rooms and centralized heating, which is nice.

It took them around 5 months of constant emailing for them to replace the rotton and moldy windowsills in our apartment. There was rot and mold from the leaky windows and the leaking AC over many years. Ithaca Renting took a LONG time to actually do something about it. Maintenance requests aren't actually addressed promptly unless it is a really easy fix (e.g. an unscrewed cupboard door). But with the price increases in rent if you can find another place to live, go live there. It is also really inconsistent, the company. I paid the same amount as a friend in plaza and all his amenities were newer, cleaner, and his rooms were 1.5x larger. And they had the most amazing view. It's honestly ridiculous their pricing and inconsistencies in quality.
Old furniture, fluctuating water temperatures, and kind of pricey for what you're getting
Good location!
Musty vibes. Old building, elevator is randomly broken. Pretty cramped space.

The value is the best part -- only around $765 for a single in the heart of collegetown. It feels a bit jank since you share two bathrooms and a kitchen with the rest of the floor, and it looks run-down on both the inside and outside, but for the price and location I'm not complaining.

Maintenance is also good. They have someone who is always on call and can come within an hour or so. There was one time where I was locked out of my apartment because the door jammed and I called but they didn't come until next morning so I slept on the couch in the common area, but besides that they've been great.

Pretty happy with the apartment, I like everything about the apartment except the location but even then the shuttle runs during the week and early during the weekend. Kind of annoying if you need to go to CTOWN during the weekend.
I enjoy the cheap, affordable living space as well as the cheap parking. However, it is too far from campus and Collegetown, and there is an extra slope to walk up in order to go anywhere.

The location is terrible especially after they discontinued the shuttle. They say they have no obligation to bring it back but have been trying since september. Their sinks are too small to do anything because they are probably 8x8 in. If you’re not careful to get an in-unit washer dryer then you have to lug your laundry down a hill and some stairs in the snow. The laundry machines ate also more expensive than on campus ones

Pretty happy with the apartment, I like everything about the apartment except the location but even then the shuttle runs during the week and early during the weekend. Kind of annoying if you need to go to CTOWN during the weekend.

The maintenance here is horrible, I send in requests and they do not get done. We didn’t have access to laundry for over three months and the shuttle only started halfway into my lease. Other than that it is decently priced compared to other apartments ($850/month)
The sinks are so comically small you can’t wash your dishes without flooding your apartment
Auden is a very clean and peaceful location and the rent is very reasonable. Also, the rooms are nice and come furnished.

This apartment is pretty satisfactory. The utilities seem to be pretty high (in the winter it is above $100). The walls and floors are super thin, however, and I can hear basically everything happening in the apartment above and next to ours... Other than that, it's a nice place to call home.
Living room is huge and the kitchen is full-sized. Since I live on lower floors, it gets a bit loud. Maintenance takes a bit longer to come. Utilities not included and hardwood floors.

This place is okay - pretty spacious rooms and living room. The only thing that really really sucks is that utilities are soooo expensive every month. We have to call NYSEG every month to negotiate our gas and electricity bill because it is so crazy high. If you want to live here, make sure to add $150 to your monthly rent and decide if it is worth it to live here through all these hidden utility charges.
Location is the only good part.
N/A
Great house,  landlord very responsive.

address: 217 Columbia St. - Unit A, Ithaca, NY 14850

reviews.text: Overall, location isn't the best (have to walk up a slight slope) but not too far from campus. Neighbors can be loud as well but that's only on certain days of the year. Really like the amount of space for the price though and the apartment layout is really roomy.
Good price for the location, all utilities included. Kinda janky!
The apartment is a little bit old, but the landlord is excellent and tries their best to accommodate all of your requests even during the day!
Great location, Good Price, Friendly owners that are very accommodating. Utilities are included. Quiet neighborhood that is perfect for grad students.

building_key: 23 min drive to cornell ithaca cortland bing 30 min drive

address: 232 S Geneva St Unit 4, Ithaca, NY

reviews.text: Lovely house but a little janky. Laundry is not convenient. Landlord is very responsive though. House gets nice light in the kitchen.

This is one of the best apartments in Collegetown. However, I have faced a few bug problems, such as a few spiders and a worm or two when it rained. Overall though, the place is clean, quiet, and has a great location.

address: 25 West main Street near union street
3 white church rd. near valley rd.

reviews.text: good location
Great location! But limited communication on paying rent and our utilities.

I've really enjoyed this apartment. I lived in an apartment of 6 and it was still quite spacious for us. My only gripes would be the kitchen is a little too small for 6 people and the drying/washer machines are kind of weak.
The place is very good but communication hasn't been the best and we had to send checks in for the first month of rent. However, it has been fine since then.
Nice property. Not super new.

The apartment itself is in a decent location and has no safety issues. Walls are concrete so noise pollution is not an issue either. The apartment seems to be very old and rusted, and some rooms lack proper ventillation and gets very dusty. Receiving mail is also inconsistent as sometimes packages go missing or letters are not there.
decent location, not highest quality apartment, carpeted

This was a super nice apartment that is very close to campus. It is located right above Jason's which is great if you ever need food immediately. It also has a great view. Rent is normal but you are paying for a nice apartment with some great maintenance from landlords and good conditions. Overall very nice living experience
Overall, great value for the cost! Very spacious.

The people who run Urban Ithaca aren't the best. But, Brad the maintenance man is a goat. The housing is definitely overpriced. And the inside is old and outdated. But, the space and location is nice.

The people who run Urban Ithaca aren't the best. But, Brad the maintenance man is a goat. The housing is definitely overpriced. And the inside is old and outdated. But, the space and location is nice.

The third floor of this place really sucks. There was absolutely no living room and the bathroom doesn’t even lock. The floors are so creaky, the roof slopes in weirdly so tall people can’t even stand up properly, and the worst part was that our fire alarm kept beeping due to lack of battery but maintenance did not come until a couple of weeks later. The walls are so thin that I could consistently hear the people who lived under me fight with their partner. Please really consider not living here.

Location is extremely hard to beat! 2 minute walk away from CTB and 3 minutes away from campus. Although the real estate company can do a better job of communicating with us about lease agreements and fines, I would say the house itself is really great for what it is.
Great apartment! Spacious and clean, great communication with landlords
Beautiful, clean apartments with a high quality build and VERY good landlord that's always on top of maintenance requests.

Great location to everything and right above Greenstar. Everything is good and the rooms are really pretty. Floors are very sturdy so you can exercise in it. Walls are kind of thin and you can hear roommates. Bathroom and kitchen facilities are average. Rooms are really big but the shared living space is smaller than expected. The major downside I have of the apartment is that I live on the college avenue side on the 2nd floor so I can hear everything outside. It is really annoying and wakes me up in the morning and keeps up at night. If it was a normal semester it would be 100x more annoying with the parties in Collegetown. It is a good apartment, but I do not think it is worth the price. Also, I would not live in the same room again because the noise from the street was too annoying. I think people in other locations of the apartment wouldn't have that complaint though.
super nice and spacious, management is super friendly and always helpful. only knock is it’s pricy

Large, spacious living room and decently sized bedrooms. Facilities are quite modern though the kitchen space is a bit on the smaller side. The only downside is the price and it can get quite noisy outside at times.
The experience is very good. The apartment looks very nice, with big rooms and big beds. The biggest issue is price, which sucks in more than 2/3 of my internship income :(((

Great location to everything and right above Greenstar. Everything is good and the rooms are really pretty. Floors are very sturdy so you can exercise in it. Walls are kind of thin and you can hear roommates. Bathroom and kitchen facilities are average. Rooms are really big but the shared living space is smaller than expected. The major downside I have of the apartment is that I live on the college avenue side on the 2nd floor so I can hear everything outside. It is really annoying and wakes me up in the morning and keeps up at night. If it was a normal semester it would be 100x more annoying with the parties in Collegetown. It is a good apartment, but I do not think it is worth the price. Also, I would not live in the same room again because the noise from the street was too annoying. I think people in other locations of the apartment wouldn't have that complaint though.
Great house with nice living area. Best street in Collegetown.

address: 308 E Seneca St Unit 2B, Ithaca, NY

reviews.text: I've had a great time overall and would recommend living here, it's just expensive but most of the time it's quiet, but the noise from neighbors still gets through. It's way more loud in the center of collegetown anyway. I would say it's one of the cleanest and well maintained buildings I saw during my tours and I enjoy living there right now.

address: 309 S Aurora Street - 2, 309 AUR S, Ithaca, NY 14850

reviews.text: Very nice place super close to campus! It’s a little expensive but it’s worth for the peace and the space. Maintenance always arrives within the next day and I have no issues with the place.

312 is amazing and has a lot of different amenities including indoor parking which I really like. There can be better furniture such as newer air conditioners but overall, a peaceful place to live. You can pick up mail during certain hours but overall, the mail hours are pretty accessible. The place is more expensive than other places in ctown.
It’s nice
Nice building! Would be nice to have updated amenities. Great view as well. The building manager is helpful
Great place to live!
Very nice place with a lot of space and a nice price. Only issue is it’s far from campus.
I like it! There’s some construction noises, but it’s a given for collegetown. It’s kinda smelly in the hallways tho.
312 College Ave is a good property, however, the construction is really loud so definitely make sure you're not facing the construction if you're planning to live here. It's also pretty pricey.
Very nice place that’s close to campus. It’s just a little expensive but the peace and space is great and I have no issues.

I've enjoyed my time living at 312 College Ave. The rent is high but you do get what you pay for. The location is good and I've had no complaints about the staff, maintenance, security, or anything like that. Utilities except for electricity are also covered. My kitchen was recently upgraded for free which is a plus. My only problem with this apartment is its price.
Great location!
Mostly quiet, only a few dormant frats nearby. This is a quick walk to the arts quad and it’s super spacious! Highly recommend if collegetown is too expensive for you!
Thurston is a great road to live on bc even tho it’s off campus it’s really close and easy to get to classes. It’s also nice being on north

address: 317-2S South Cayuga St near E. Clinton St

reviews.text: Lambrou is standard. I’d recommend, but sometimes they can be a bit silent. As long as you walk into the physical building they’re very responsive. Would recommend. A great location.

address: 317.5 E. Buffalo Street near Parker St.

reviews.text: Apartment is super nice and great location. The Maintenance is pretty subpar, they tried to fix our AC units but then stopped after a while and gave us room fans. They also take a while for maintenance requests. The landlord is also pretty odd.
Good place, decent landlord.
Great location and price but must compromise for poor management and run down apartments
Pretty good housing for the price and location. It's a bit loud because of the bars nearby.
Great location!
I live here and I love the location (~3 mins from campus)! Great price for Ithaca and rooms are spacious

I have not yet lived at my apartment for long now but hearing from others, he is extremely strict about getting back the money from our security deposits and purposefully overcharges us for minuscule things. In addition, he has been disregarding the Ithaca freeze or even the option to use our security deposit to pay the monthly rent, even though it was a state mandated order.

The landlord is pretty nice, and the location and living conditions are decent. The views outside the window are not great though, and the price is a bit steep (probably because of the location). Rent needs to be paid via check, which is inconvenient if you're not in Ithaca. Trash and laundry are not included in the rent, and if you need parking you need to buy a spot through a third party.
Condition not good

Renting at this location has been my best rental experience in Ithaca! The biggest pro is that it's an excellent value - the property is so well renovated and taken care of that living in the unit is seamless, and the proximity to several locations cannot be beaten.

Renting at this location has been my best rental experience in Ithaca! The biggest pro is that it's an excellent value - the property is so well renovated and taken care of that living in the unit is seamless, and the proximity to several locations cannot be beaten.
Clean apartments!! Amazing location above 7//11 (only thing is slow elevator haha)
Great location, good space in the 4 bedroom apartments
The location is very close to campus, so it's convenient. The space isn't that big, but it's comfortable. The walls are quite soundproof.

This apartment has a great location and is located right next to the UPS and 711 in Collegetown. The conditions of the apartment are pretty good and it is very easy to have the space feel home-y. The kitchen space is big and the living room space also comes with a TV. The landlord responds very fast but maintenance requests take a while to get fixed.
Overall good, sometimes maintenance is slow but they always respond.

We have a 4 bedroom with 2 baths on the second floor away from the Main Street so it’s very quiet. Decent place and quality. Landlords have been pretty good to interact with. Slightly pricey but it’s just above 711 so it’s close to campus and is in the middle of ctown.

actually really enjoyed living in 407! absolutely scrumptious location and the price isn't that much worse than other parts of collegetown. it's right next to 7/11 which is super convenient. loved the large amount of counter space (basically had a kitchen island) and having 2 bathrooms. also it's naturally crusty unlike some other apts. sometimes the elevator breaks and the dryers are kinda bad at drying but that's like the worst of it. would def live here again and highly recommend it
Great location - it’s right above DP Dough.

the best part about aces apartment is the location and it’s proximity to everything in ctown and campus, but the building itself is extremely old and you should definitely keep this in mind when deciding to move in or not! the rooms are also very different in size and quality (noise-wise as well) so keep this in mind!!

Overall, I have enjoyed my time living here. The location makes campus and other areas in Collegetown incredibly accessible, and it’s fun living in the hub of Cornell. Having e-hub and an in-building gym is very convenient as well. However, there are periodic maintenance issues (infrequent enough) that arise, including building key and elevator issues. Some downsides of the building include thin walls, windows that don’t open, and no windows in the bedrooms (unless you have a studio). However, the appliances are very new and the space is great for hosting company. It definitely feels like upscale living in Ithaca.
Maintenance is really fast and the staff are really nice. The only problem is the price of rent.

I would say that the location for Student Agencies is probably the best because it's closest to campus. The apartment is pretty clean and new with study lounges and a gym. However, I don't know if it's worth the price. There are no dishwashers and my bedroom has no windows too.

address: 412 E Marshall St - Unit 1, Ithaca, NY 14850
414 E Marshall St - 414 East Marshall 1 Bed open now , 2 beds out of 4 Open for 2026-2027, Ithaca, NY 14850

reviews.text: This apartment is generally very old. There are some wear-downs in the apartment, but the maintenance service is relatively quick and communicates well. There is not much ventilation in the apartment so it gets quite hot in the summer. The rent is ok, not too high not too low. There is a deli right downstairs (very convenient for snacks :) )
it’s a fine place to live! about as expected for the price, a bit run down but cheap and great location

address: 419 East Buffalo Street near 419 East Buffalo Street

reviews.text: I have really enjoyed living in this house! It is a great place for a large group of friends, and a very cozy house! Totally recommend living here if you are up for a little bit of a commute to campus (off of North). Also a very affordable price!

address: 455 West Dryden Rd near Sheldon Rd
457 West Dryden Rd near Wood rd
504 Stewart Ave Cascadilla Gorge Penthouse, Ithaca, NY

reviews.text: Overall, the apartment itself is spacious and in a good location a 15 min walk to campus. It had the potential to be a good apt because of its space and location but all the other issues made the experience really bad. The landlord was often rude and not understanding; she made us pay 7 months of rent (2 months security deposit and 5 months shortly after) before our lease started and before we even got the keys. At the time, the pandemic had just started and everything was up in the air, and we didn't know what our plans were, and she was threatening to press charges a whole week before the 5 months of rent was even due.

The landlord is very hard to deal with. When I first moved in she said she cleaned the apartment and shampooed the carpets before our move in date, but it was very clear that she did neither; the carpets had large stains all over them and the apartment didn't look like it had been cleaned anytime recently. In the begining of the fall semester, she scheduled a tour without letting us know and people just randomly and unexpectedly walked into the apartment. Besides that, the apartment is not well maintained. It needs new furniture and the carpets need to be cleaned. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

The landlord is very hard to deal with. When I first moved in she said she cleaned the apartment and shampooed the carpets before our move in date, but it was very clear that she did neither; the carpets had large stains all over them and the apartment didn't look like it had been cleaned anytime recently. In the begining of the fall semester, she scheduled a tour without letting us know and people just randomly and unexpectedly walked into the apartment. Besides that, the apartment is not well maintained. It needs new furniture and the carpets need to be cleaned. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

address: 5120 Halseyville road. near Perry City road

reviews.text: Ravenwood is a decent option if you want to find an affordable place to live off campus. The price is much cheaper than a lot of the other off-campus options (around $790 a month per person, and the landlord is open to negotiating the price), the rooms are pretty big, and the amenities are alright, although the quality seems to vary a lot between different apartments, so definitely take a look at the apartment before signing the lease. Maintenance in my experience, is pretty good, and the work people will come within a few days of submitting a request. The amount of maintenance needed though, is not great, and you will probably need to submit at least one request during your stay. The biggest downside is the walk to central. Ravenwood is located further downhill from West, so you will have to walk uphill to West and then up the slope to get to class. The area is also not the safest. My roommate had some of his stuff stolen over Thanksgiving break and the Ithaca police couldn't do much because there isn't any surveillance in the area (although the landlord is working on fixing this). The walls are also pretty thin, so sometimes you can hear people in the living room or in apartments above/to the side, which can be distracting. Overall, the living conditions are very good for the cost, but there are definitely some big downsides.

Ravenwood is very affordable compared to its collegetown counterparts, and its more remote location means a very quiet and spacious apartment. However, the walk to anywhere on campus or collegetown is substantive and uphill. The apartment itself is, like I said, spacious, but the conditions are not great--some things are in disrepair. Maintenance is pretty responsive, especially for urgent issues. Laundry room is decently located and well maintained, but it is a walk outdoors to get to it. I've never felt particularly unsafe around the area, because it is remote compared to collegetown. I think the main benefits are the spacious apartment and quiet location, while the conditions reflect the price that you pay for. It is a great option if you don't mind the distance from campus and collegetown.

It’s slightly down the slope but at least it’s close to West Campus and right next to a bus stop. The building itself is fine, and the office is reachable during working hours. Maintenance takes a while. Overall, it’s a good spot.

The location isn't ideal and well there are definitely many more places that are in better condition/have better facilities, but the apartment is big and I get to live with many other friends and we have a fairly big living room that we play games in on a daily basis so I dont regret my choice.
Easy access to laundry and trash. Utilities included, which is a plus. Maintenance is really easy to get done and payments can be made online. Overall great landlord / apartment. Also pet friendly!

address: 522 E State St near Sage Pl
523 E State St near Stewart Ave.
54 shudaben rd near Enfield ctr rd
601 E. State St Unit H, Ithaca, NY
605 S. Aurora St. Unit 2, Ithaca, NY
605 S. Aurora St. House, Ithaca, NY
612 E State St Unit 2, Ithaca, NY
613 E State St - Whole Complex, Ithaca, NY 14850

reviews.text: Homey rooms, spacious living area, maintenance comes often to fix things up quickly which is great! Overall, would recommend (the hill walk isn’t too bad, you’ll get used to it)

address: 635 bone plain rd near scofield rd
702 E. State St Unit B, Ithaca, NY
704 E. State St Unit AF, Ithaca, NY
707 E. Seneca St Unit 2B, Ithaca, NY

reviews.text: It’s been a good college apartment experience. The price matched the quality of the building. The landlords respond quickly to any issues. Good experience overall.

Loved living at campus hill for 2 years in undergrad! It was a newly renovated unit with hardwood floors. My only complaint was kitchen size because it was kind of cramped. Management is really nice, accommodating, and responsive. Compared to some of the other Landlords in Ithaca, campus hill was better by far! Location is also super nice since it’s right next to the west campus dorms. Only a 5 minute hike up the slope to central campus!

address: 710 E State St - 710 House, ithaca, NY 14850

reviews.text: I would sum up my experience as: serviceable. Do I want to live her long-term? Definitely not, but for a couple semesters, it'll do. The landlord is not the worst I've had, though she could be better and the rent price is fair.
Very practice and meets all requirements could be better especially in cleanliness

This apartment is very nice and newly renovated! The management is professional and kind. My only gripes are that it could be a little larger or a little cheaper per month. Also sometimes maintenance isn’t responsive.

address: 711 E. Seneca St Unit 3, Ithaca, NY

reviews.text: Lovely apartment, far-ish from campus though
Good overall. Rent increase drastically which was main issue with property.

address: 815 S Aurora St - 3 Bedroom, Ithaca, NY 14850
826 N Aurora St near 826 N Aurora St
